description: Collection of decompiled/saved playable ad HTML files from real production ads. Includes 7 complete single-file HTML playable ads covering multiple game genres: Empires & Puzzles (match3/RPG), Idle Theme Park (idle tycoon), Slots, Virus shooter, and Build & Fest. Also contains video ad examples and Python scripts for parsing ad media JSON.
notes: Files are large (1-2.7MB each) bundled production ads. Useful as reference for output format and CTA patterns but not as source code templates.

description: Professional webpack-based build system for playable ads. Provides CLI tools to compile TypeScript/JavaScript playable ad projects into single-file HTML bundles optimized for 20+ ad networks. Includes network-specific injector plugins (MRAID, DAPI, TikTok, Pangle, Mintegral, ExitAPI), ZIP packaging, inline asset bundling, and per-network HTML template configurations. Companion tool to @smoud/playable-sdk.
patterns: asset inline as base64 (images, audio, fonts, 3D models)
notes: Production-grade build system. Supports 21 ad networks. Excellent reference for understanding the build pipeline and per-network requirements for playable ads.

description: TypeScript SDK providing a unified API for playable ad lifecycle management across all major ad networks.
notes: Best-in-class SDK for playable ad abstraction. Clean TypeScript code with comprehensive ad network support.

description: A complete HTML5 Canvas-based 'Buca' puck/ball physics puzzle game.
notes: Student-level project. No MRAID/ad integration, no CTA button.

description: A 3D bubble shooter playable ad built with Cocos Creator 3.x.
patterns: BFS flood fill for same-color matching
notes: Well-architected Cocos Creator project. Excellent reference for bubble shooter mechanics.

description: Build tool that converts Cocos Creator 2.x web-mobile builds into single-HTML playable ad files.
patterns: Cocos Creator 2.x web-mobile to single HTML
notes: Targets Cocos Creator 2.x (cc.loader API).

description: Python-based build tool for converting Cocos Creator 2.x/3.x web-mobile builds into single-HTML playable ads.
notes: Good reference for the complete asset loading pipeline from an in-memory resource map.

description: Comprehensive Gulp-based build pipeline for converting Defold game engine projects into single-HTML playable ads with MRAID support.
notes: Most sophisticated build tool in the collection. 2MB file size warning aligns with ad network limits.

description: A complete PixiJS-based slot machine playable ad. Features 3-reel slot machine with spinning animation, item reveal sequence, fire ray win effect, jackpot popup with animated number counter, tutorial overlay, responsive portrait/landscape layout with screen type detection (S/M/L), and a custom rendering framework built on top of PixiJS with declarative config-driven scene composition. Uses tween.js for all animations including bounce easing on slot movement.
patterns: webpack builder for single-file HTML output
notes: Excellent PixiJS architecture with a reusable core rendering framework (Application, RenderElement, RenderElementFactory, Tweens). The declarative config system for scene composition is a great pattern. Shared core with merge-game repo. Includes a webpack-based builder for single-file HTML output.

description: A complete PixiJS-based merge puzzle playable ad. Features a grid of cells with draggable items that can be merged when matching types. Includes drag-and-drop with collision detection, merge animation with color-tinted particle effects, progress bar with timer, fail/win popups, counter display, and blackout overlay. Uses the same core rendering framework as casino-slots with declarative config-driven scene composition.
patterns: animated sprite particle effects on merge
progress bar with timer (fail on timeout)
drag holder for re-parenting during drag
webpack builder for single-file HTML output
notes: Excellent merge game implementation with clean drag-and-drop mechanics. The GridController + Item + Cell architecture is a great reference for grid-based merge games. Shares core framework with casino-slots. Includes webpack builder.

description: A Phaser 3 cat-themed tycoon/builder playable ad. Player starts with a tutorial showing a cat character and speech bubbles, then builds and upgrades 3 different buildings (Dryer, Resting Area, Shredder) by collecting money from timer-based income bubbles. Features JSON-driven level configuration, sprite sheet animations for each building with normal/upgrade/loop states, hammer construction animation, download button CTA, particle effects (stars, money, confetti), money panel UI, pointer tutorial system, and isometric-style building placement.
patterns: building level-up system with particle effects
custom button component with pressed state
canvas resize with aspect ratio scaling
notes: Good Phaser 3 tycoon pattern. The Building class with upgrade animations and the TimerCircle income system are useful references. Heavy reliance on JSON config for positioning makes it data-driven. No MRAID integration but has CTA button.

description: A comprehensive 3D driving/delivery playable ad built with Cocos Creator 3.x. Player controls a vehicle via touch-drag to collect resources from ice blocks and deliver them to a home point. Features temperature/health system that decreases outside home radius, vehicle upgrade system (person -> car -> snow truck), ice block breaking with physics-based collision, resource stacking on vehicle with parabolic pickup animation, sequential resource delivery animation, frozen material effect, camera rotation on victory, guide UI tutorial, and complete manager architecture (Game, UI, Audio, Camera, Effect, Material, Model, Physics, Resource, Animation, Screen managers).
patterns: UI manager with typed UI enum
audio manager with BGM and loop SFX
notes: Most comprehensive Cocos Creator 3.x project in the collection. Excellent manager architecture with 11 separate manager classes. The PlayerController alone demonstrates touch raycasting, temperature system, resource collection, vehicle upgrading, and delivery mechanics. Includes playable-ads-adapter extension for single-HTML export.

description: A mini golf playable ad built with Phaser 3 + Matter.js physics. Player taps the ball, aims by dragging, and releases to shoot. Features drag-to-aim with variable power indicator (4 levels), Matter.js physics with bounce and friction, polygon border collision via physics shapes loaded from JSON, hole detection, step counter with best result tracking, window.eventTracker integration for ad analytics (ongamestart/ongameover/turn), and responsive resizing.
notes: Clean mini golf implementation with proper physics. The Ball class demonstrates a complete drag-to-aim-and-shoot mechanic. The Sizer utility for responsive scaling is reusable. Has ad event tracking hooks but no specific network integration.

description: A build tool/template for creating Phaser 3 playable ads with Spine animation support. Features a custom SpineBase64Plugin that allows loading Spine skeleton JSON and atlas files from base64-encoded inline data (avoiding external file loading). Includes tools for generating base64-encoded assets, inlining the bundle into a single HTML file, and packing with pako compression. The demo game shows a Spine-animated bee with bitmap font text.
patterns: Phaser 3 Spine plugin with base64 loading
notes: Useful specifically for the SpineBase64Plugin pattern - loading Spine animations from inline base64 data in playable ads. The inline-bundle.js tool is minimal but effective.

description: A Homescapes-style playable ad built with PixiJS and pixi-ease. Player is presented with old stairs in a home renovation scene. Clicking a hammer reveals 3 menu options for new stair designs. Selecting one shows the new stair, clicking OK shows the final renovation result with an overlay. Features sprite atlas loading, interactive buttons with hover states, ease-based animations (slide, fade, scale, strobe), and responsive aspect-ratio-based resizing.
patterns: selection confirmation flow (hammer -> options -> OK -> result)
notes: Good Homescapes-style renovation playable pattern. The menu selection -> preview -> confirm flow is a common playable ad mechanic. Single-file JS implementation.

description: Another Homescapes-style playable ad built with PixiJS and Charm animation library. Same renovation concept: hammer triggers 3 pad options for stairs, selecting one shows the new stair with component-by-component reveal animation (stair base, holders, carpet), OK button shows final result overlay. Uses a data-driven approach with scene items defined in a separate data.js file. Features webpack bundling and the Charm tween library for strobe/slide/fade/scale animations.
notes: Alternative Homescapes implementation with data-driven scene composition. The Charm library is lighter than GSAP. Good reference for pad selection UI pattern.

description: A Fruit Ninja-style playable ad built with PixiJS and CreateJS TweenJS. Fruits of 6 types (blue/green/orange/purple/red/yellow) spawn from screen edges with parabolic trajectories. Player slices by swiping/dragging across fruits, which split into left/right halves with rotation. Features 60-second timer with progressive difficulty (spawn interval decreases over time), score system based on fruit color values, game over panel, and touch/mouse input for slicing detection.
patterns: fruit parabolic trajectory (sineOut up, sineIn down)
notes: Clean fruit ninja implementation. The Fruit class demonstrates parabolic trajectory + split animation. Progressive difficulty with decreasing spawn intervals is a good playable ad pattern for increasing engagement.

description: A professional-quality garden makeover/farm playable ad with TWO implementations: one in plain HTML/CSS/JS with Three.js, and one in TypeScript with PixiJS + Three.js. Features 3D garden scene with slot-based item placement (fence, tomato, corn, sheep + bonus items), drag-to-place mechanic with ghost preview, task system (required + bonus items), coin/diamond economy with spend/refund, undo system, day/night toggle with lighting changes, tutorial overlay, VFX (smoke puffs, pop animations), GLTF model loading with cache, audio system, MRAID CTA integration, and comprehensive event bus architecture.
patterns: GLTF model loading with template cache
audio system (theme BGM, click SFX, place SFX)
typed EventBus with strict event map
frame rate cap for low-end devices
notes: Best-in-class playable ad architecture. The dual implementation (plain JS and TS+PixiJS+Three.js) makes it excellent for studying patterns. The typed EventBus, StateManager, PlacementSystem with raycasting, and slot-based placement are production-quality. Has MRAID CTA integration. Most architecturally sophisticated game code in the entire collection.

description: A 3D action/survival playable ad built with Cocos Creator 3.x. Player controls a character via mouse/touch to move, harvest resources (wood/stone) with weapon attacks, upgrade weapons at crafting benches, and break through gates. Features ray-based mouse-to-ground aiming, attack animation with hitbox overlap detection, 3-level weapon progression, crafting bench proximity UI, gate breaking mechanic, resource collection with UI counters, and game state management (start menu/in-game/pause).
notes: Good Cocos Creator 3.x action game pattern. The PlayerController demonstrates raycasting, attack hitbox, resource harvesting, weapon upgrades, and collision avoidance in one class. The CraftingBench proximity UI is a useful interaction pattern.

description: A polished Homescapes-style renovation playable ad built with PixiJS and GSAP. Features a character (Austin), environment scene with furniture, stair renovation with 3 options, menu trigger system, bounce/fade stair animations, dark overlay for result display, and responsive scaling. Uses Vite for building, GSAP for smooth animations, and a clean modular architecture with separate classes for App, Environment, Stairs, Menu, MenuTrigger, and Overlay.
patterns: scene offset and scale for responsive layout
notes: The cleanest Homescapes implementation in the collection. Good modular architecture with GSAP for smooth animations. The Sizes helper calculates viewport/scene dimensions for responsive layout. Better code quality than pixijs_playableAd and playableAd versions of the same concept.

description: A minimal Flappy Bird-style playable ad demo for Facebook Audience Network. Simple canvas game where a red square flies through green pillar obstacles using gravity + acceleration button. Includes FbPlayableAd.onCTAClick() for the 'Buy Me' CTA button. Pure vanilla JavaScript with setInterval game loop, AABB collision detection, and gravity physics.
notes: Minimal but demonstrates the key FbPlayableAd.onCTAClick() pattern for Facebook playable ads. The game itself is extremely basic (colored rectangles, no sprites) but the CTA integration pattern is valuable.

description: A simple planet clicker playable ad with HTML/CSS/JS. Features image-swapping between different planetary states triggered by setTimeout timing. No actual clicking mechanic despite the name - just sequential image reveals (ellipse -> bang1 -> bang2 -> bang3). Pure DOM manipulation without canvas or game framework.
notes: Very minimal - just timed image swapping. No real game logic, no interaction beyond watching. Only useful as a template for the simplest possible playable ad structure.

description: A bundled single-file HTML playable ad for an empire/brawl game (~1.8MB). Production-quality bundled output with inline base64 assets. The file is minified and not human-readable as source code.
notes: Bundled/minified production ad. Not useful as source reference - only as an example of final output format. File is 1.8MB.

description: A bundled single-file HTML virus shooter playable ad (~1.7MB). Production-quality bundled output with Laya engine. The file is minified/obfuscated and loads external library scripts via loadLib function.
notes: Bundled/minified production ad using Laya engine. Not useful as source reference. File is 1.7MB.

description: A bundled single-file HTML skins/character customization playable ad (~2.4MB). Production-quality with MRAID integration, download button CTA, orientation change handling, and viewable state management. Uses createSprite factory pattern for sprite creation. Contains base64-encoded assets inline.
notes: Bundled production ad with MRAID integration. The MRAID lifecycle code (ready/viewableChange) is extractable as a reference pattern. File is 2.4MB.

description: Empty repository with only a README.md file. No game code.
notes: Empty repo - no code to analyze.

description: A Cocos Creator 2.x to single-HTML playable ad build tool. Nearly identical to fkworld/cocos-to-playable-ad - converts web-mobile build output into a single HTML file by encoding all resources as base64 into window.res, injecting CSS (clean-css minified) and JS inline, and overriding cc.loader download handlers to serve from the in-memory resource map. Handles png/jpg/webp/mp3 as base64, other files as text.
patterns: Cocos Creator 2.x web-mobile to single HTML
base64 encoding for images and audio
notes: Fork/variant of fkworld/cocos-to-playable-ad with identical architecture. Same pattern: window.res map + custom cc.loader handlers. Useful as additional reference for the Cocos 2.x bundling pattern.

description: A Unity/Luna basketball throwing playable ad. The project is a Unity project converted via Luna Playable (luna.json, LunaTemp/). Contains C# source code already parsed in previous batch. The JS/JSON files are Luna build output and diagnostic files, not hand-written game code.
notes: Unity C# project converted via Luna. The JS files are auto-generated Luna output, not useful as hand-written code reference. Skip for HTML5/JS analysis.

description: A Unity/Luna playable ad demo. Same structure as PlayableAd_BasketBallThrowing - Unity project converted via Luna Playable. Contains C# source already parsed. JS/JSON files are auto-generated Luna build output.
notes: Unity C# project converted via Luna. Auto-generated JS output, not useful as code reference.

description: A Unity Tiny (Project Tiny/DOTS) racing playable ad. The TinyRacing.html is a Unity WebGL/Tiny build output containing Emscripten-generated JavaScript with a TinyEventManager for input handling. The game logic is compiled from C#/DOTS to WebAssembly. Not hand-written JavaScript.
notes: Unity Tiny compiled output. The HTML shell has TinyEventManager input system but core game logic is in WASM. Not useful as hand-written JS reference.

description: Empty or minimal repository for a Cocos 3.6.x playable ads build tool. No source files found.
notes: Empty/minimal repo with no actual source code to analyze.
